title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: High blood pressure or hypertension is a common condition in which the force of blood on

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: the walls of your arteries is often too high.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Arteries are the blood vessels that carry blood away from your heart to supply your tissues

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: with oxygen and nutrients.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: In your heart, two chambers, called ventricles, contract with each heartbeat to push blood

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: to your lungs and through your arteries to your body.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: As blood flows through them, three main factors affect the pressure on your artery walls.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: The first is cardiac output or the amount of blood your ventricles push out of your

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: heart each minute.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Your blood pressure goes up as cardiac output increases.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: The second factor affecting your blood pressure is blood volume or the total amount of blood

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: in your body.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Blood pressure also goes up as blood volume increases.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: The third factor that affects your blood pressure is resistance, which is anything working

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: against the blood flow through your arteries.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Several factors contribute to resistance.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: One resistance factor is the flexibility of your artery wall.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Healthy arteries expand with each heartbeat to help reduce blood pressure on the wall.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Another resistance factor is the diameter of your arteries.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Your body is able to increase the diameter of your arteries to lower your blood pressure

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: or reduce the diameter to raise your blood pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: A third resistance factor is blood viscosity or thickness.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: In your blood, more particles, such as proteins and fat, increase viscosity.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: If your blood is thicker, your blood pressure goes up as your heart works harder to push

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: it through your arteries.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Your blood pressure can be measured with a device called a Sphigmomanometer or blood pressure

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: cuff.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: When your heart beats, the pressure of blood on the walls of your arteries is called systolic

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: When your heart relaxes between beats, pressure on the artery wall is called diastolic pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: While your blood pressure may change throughout the day, it should optimally be less than 120

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: millimeters of mercury for systolic pressure and less than 80 millimeters of mercury for

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: diastolic pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: If your systolic pressure frequently stays above 130 or your diastolic pressure frequently

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: stays above 80, you have high blood pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Your time, high blood pressure will damage the walls of your arteries.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Your artery wall may become weak and form an enlargement called an aneurysm or the wall

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: may burst and bleed into the surrounding tissue.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Small tears in your artery wall may attract certain substances in your blood, such as cholesterol,

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: fat and calcium to form a buildup called a plaque.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Blood flow through your artery decreases as the plaque enlarges.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Blood cells can stick to the plaque and form solid clumps called clots, further reducing

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: or completely blocking your blood flow.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Damage to your arteries raises your blood pressure even more by making your heart beat

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: more forcefully.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Artery damage and reduced blood flow lead to conditions such as a stroke, heart attack

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: or kidney disease.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: In most cases, the cause of high blood pressure or hypertension is unknown.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: This type of high blood pressure is called primary or essential hypertension.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Treatment for essential hypertension includes lifestyle changes, such as eating a healthy

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: diet.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: If you are sensitive to the sodium in salt, your doctor may recommend limiting your intake

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: of salt and highly processed foods.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Your doctor may cause your body to retain water, which increases both your blood volume

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: and your blood pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Other lifestyle changes that can reduce blood pressure include avoiding excessive alcohol

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: intake, getting regular exercise, losing weight if you are overweight, and quitting smoking,

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: and your doctor may also recommend medications that act on your kidneys, blood vessels,

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: or heart to help reduce your blood pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Diuretics commonly called water pills cause your kidneys to move more salt and water

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: from your blood into your urine, which reduces your blood volume and pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Beta blockers reduce the workload on your heart by decreasing both the rate of your heart

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: beat and the strength of your heart's contractions.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: Several types of drugs act directly or indirectly to reduce your blood pressure by relaxing your

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: blood vessels, which increases their diameter.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: These drugs include ACE inhibitors, angiotensin-2 receptor blockers, calcium channel blockers,

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
text_en: and direct acting vasodilators.
